So like, obviously, I don't want to do this to campaigns that are paused or turned off. Nothing is going to happen. It's just kind of dumb. And I'm going to add another condition, which is simply going to be conversions. My cost per conversion is less. So let's say if my cost per conversion is less than or equals, let's say, I don't know, $13, I want to apply this. All right. So that'll be great. That'll start scaling it if I get if I start getting cheaper conversions, which I fully expect to do, by the way, because the longer you run campaigns, the smarter the machine learning on the ad platforms gets. So I do expect this to happen sometimes. But what's essentially going to be happening is these campaigns are going to get me conversions, you know, between at a maximum of 19 bucks or else they'll die. And if they're doing under $13, then this is going to scale it. And that's pretty sweet. Right. So let me swap over here to my little time zone here. And I'm going to do from 12am to 1am. That's when that's going to run. I'm going to say from yesterday, not today, dang it, yesterday. And I'm going to say no emails. All right. So we've got the exact same thing going on here. So this is very, very helpful. This is like having some little OCD person checking your ad stats all the time and scaling them or dropping them down for you. And really, the like Google is so cool that assuming you've set up your tracking properly and the stats are, you know, pretty dang accurate, they're off like by one or two because of their little delay or whatever. But assuming you've set that stuff up properly, this is kind of all there is to it. And you can just walk away from this and know you're going to get conversions within this acceptable range pretty much all the time. Now, one thing I will tell you is some days, you know, it could happen where it all just goes terribly on one day and you're getting extremely expensive conversions. And if that happens, it'll lower your budgets down. And conversely, you might have a day where like unbelievably, you know, you're getting really, really good pricing on your conversions. And as a result, it'll scale your budgets up. What you need to do is be emotionally detached from all of that stuff and just know that it can differ every day. Don't freak yourself out by looking at these results every day. You want to look at them over time, like how was this week's average cost per conversion and things like that. And the reason we set this up, incidentally, is because Google is pretty smart. So if it sees you lowering that budget, that's a signal into their machine learning going, Hey, we're not happy with this result, work harder. And it actually does. So it kind of makes it like readjust and recalibrate and like, Oh, damn, okay, let's see, let's go out here and see if we can do a little bit better. So that's why it makes sense to be raising and lowering budgets constantly with these automation. So that's kind of a really simple, super secret scaling system. I deliberately used all of those words with S's in a row. So it would sound cool. Super secret scaling system. Secrets.biz. All right, see you in the next series.
